verses:
hari he dayāla mora jaya rādhānātha
bārabāra ei bāra laha nija sātha

bahu joni bhrami’ nātha, loinu śaraṇa
nija-guṇe kṛpā kara adhamatāraṇa

jagata⋅kāraṇa tumi jagata⋅jīvana
tomā chāḍā kā’ra nahi, he rādhā-ramaṇa

bhuvanamaṅgala tumi bhuvanera pati
tumi upekṣile nātha ki hoibe gati

bhāviyā dekhinu ei jagata⋅mājhāre
tomā vinā keha nāhi e dāse uddhāre
en_translation:
O Lord Hari, my merciful one! All glories to the Lord of Rādhā! This time, please take me as Your own companion.

Having wandered through many species, O Lord, I have finally taken Your shelter. By Your own mercy, please be kind, O deliverer of the fallen.

You are the cause of the universe, and You are the life of the universe. Without You, I belong to no one, O enjoyer of Rādhā.

You are the welfare of all worlds, and You are their Lord. If You neglect me, O Lord, what will be my fate?

Having thought it over, I have realized that in this world, no one can deliver this servant but You.
